author: Adam Coddington
description: Unlock your automation needs by interacting with your notes over a secure REST API.

manifest.author: Adam Coddington
manifest.description: A secure REST API and Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for your vault.
manifest.name: Local REST API with MCP

name: Local REST API with MCP

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: - Improves the search_query MCP tool description to include a concrete NoteJson example, an inline JsonLogic operator cheatsheet, additional query examples, and a pointer to vault_read for inspecting real vault data.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Fixes a 500 error when PATCHing a document whose YAML frontmatter contains
  values valid in Obsidian but invalid per strict YAML 1.2 (e.g. an unquoted
  colon in a scalar). The REST PATCH endpoints and MCP vault_patch tool now
  return HTTP 400 with error code 40005 (InvalidFrontmatter) so callers know
  the document's frontmatter needs to be fixed.
- Bumps markdown-patch from 1.0.0 to 1.1.0, which adds FrontmatterParseError,
  fixes silent ignoring of rejectIfContentPreexists for application/json table
  content, handles empty frontmatter blocks, and fixes a bare Error thrown on
  type mismatch.

This is a re-release of 4.1.5. That release contained a TypeScript compilation
error caused by ErrorCode.InvalidFrontmatter being added to the enum without a
corresponding entry in ERROR_CODE_MESSAGES. The underlying behavior is otherwise
identical. Recurrence is prevented by a new constants.test.ts that asserts every
ErrorCode value has a message entry, and by adding typecheck + test steps to the
release CI workflow.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Fixes a 500 error when PATCHing a document whose YAML frontmatter contains
  values valid in Obsidian but invalid per strict YAML 1.2 (e.g. an unquoted
  colon in a scalar). The REST PATCH endpoints and MCP vault_patch tool now
  return HTTP 400 with error code 40005 (InvalidFrontmatter) so callers know
  the document's frontmatter needs to be fixed.
- Bumps markdown-patch from 1.0.0 to 1.1.0, which adds FrontmatterParseError,
  fixes silent ignoring of rejectIfContentPreexists for application/json table
  content, handles empty frontmatter blocks, and fixes a bare Error thrown on
  type mismatch.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: - Fixes MCP vault_patch storing application/json content as a quoted string. (#268; Thanks @psalkowski!)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Fixes authenticated path traversal vulnerability in /vault/{path} endpoints
  (GET, PUT, POST, PATCH, DELETE, MOVE). A percent-encoded slash (%2F) in the
  path could escape the vault root and allow arbitrary host file read, write,
  or delete with the privileges of the Obsidian process. (GHSA-62gx-5q78-wrvx;
  Thanks @AgenticWizard for the responsible disclosure!)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: - Fixes MCP server hanging on all but the most-recently-connected session. (#267; Thanks @psalkowski!)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: - Renames plugin to "Local REST API with MCP" to comply with Obsidian plugin directory naming guidelines.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Adds "move" operation via MOVE HTTP verb. (#191; Thanks @mairas!)
- Fixes release workflow not extracting annotated tag message.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Fixes a crash when accessing periodic notes.
- Fixes an issue where unexpected handler errors could cause the server to hang; they now return HTTP 500.
- Adds MOVE method support in CORS policy.
- Fixes an O(N²) performance issue in JSON Logic search backlinks scanning.
- Skips file content reads during search when the query doesn't reference content, improving search performance.
- Closes all connections on server shutdown and settings refresh, preventing stale connection issues.
- Configures server keep-alive timeouts to prevent stale connections. (#253; Thanks @fa1k3!)
- Fixes missing trailing slash on MCP URLs shown in the settings UI.
- Switches API documentation from Swagger UI to Stoplight Elements.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Release 4.0.2

- Fixes GitHub release workflow to set an explicit release title, resolving
  a validation error from the Obsidian community release checker.
- Removes vendorized Swagger UI docs from the repository.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Release 4.0.1

- Removes the deprecated 2.x PATCH format (Heading / Content-Insertion-Position headers) that was sunset at 4.0. All PATCH requests now require the 3.x Target-Type, Operation, and Target headers.
- Updates the release workflow to use `gh release create` with `--notes-from-tag`, removing deprecated release actions.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Release 4.0.0

- Adds a built-in Streamable HTTP MCP server at /mcp/ for use with AI assistants.
- Adds configurable logging behind an Advanced Settings toggle; by default, nothing is logged.
- Adds links and backlinks to file metadata responses.
- Adds API version negotiation and a plugin extension API for third-party plugins to register MCP tools.
- Adds artifact attestations to the release workflow.
- Upgrades to markdown-patch 1.0.0, adding the targetScope parameter to vault patch operations and renaming Apply-If-Content-Preexists to Reject-If-Content-Preexists. This is a backward-incompatible change — users relying on Apply-If-Content-Preexists will need to migrate to Reject-If-Content-Preexists.
- Removes Dataview DQL search support. This is a backward-incompatible change — users relying on DQL queries should migrate to JsonLogic.
- Fixes popout window compatibility by using activeWindow timer functions.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Release 3.6.2

- Fixes route conflict causing 400 errors on date-based periodic note paths. (#242)
- Updates markdown-patch dependency to 0.4.5, fixing section boundary detection
  that could corrupt files containing markdown tables with code-span heading
  references (e.g. `## Heading` inside a table cell).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Release 3.6.1

- Fixes GET /vault/ returning an error instead of an empty list on an empty vault.
- Fixes PATCH append-under-heading inserting an extra blank line before content. (#231)
- Adds better type guards and error handling around patch operations.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Release 3.6.0

- Sub-document sections are now fully addressable via the API. Any heading,
  block reference, or frontmatter field can be read, replaced, or appended
  to directly — without fetching or rewriting the whole file — using either
  request headers (Target-Type / Target) or by embedding the target in the
  URL path itself (e.g. /vault/note.md/heading/My%20Section). Targeting is
  now supported across GET, PUT, POST, and PATCH. (#226, Thanks @grimlor!
  #233)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
New Features:
- Increases maximum incoming payload size to 1GB from the
  express-defined default of 100kB.
- Fixes handling of incoming binary content such that you can PUT binary
  files into your vault -- just be sure to set an appropriate
  `Content-Type` header.

Backward-incompatible Changes:
- Removes /search/gui/ endpoint -- this endpoint was brittle and relied
  on private/undocumented behavior (see #71).  This functionality was
  removed due to not being maintanable long-term.

Note: this is a re-release of 2.0.0 following a handful of release build process fixes.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- #13: Fixes bug in handling of non-ascii filenames.
- #15: Fixes bug causing PATCH to a newly-created periodic note to complete unsuccessfully.
- #10: Fixes bug preventing user from updating insecure HTTP port
  (thanks @ericzinnikas!).
